ℹ️ Recent review info
⚙️ Run configuration **Configuration used**: defaults **Review profile**: CHILL **Plan**: Pro Plus **Run ID**: `b6239fc0-12ac-4d2e-b1ab-c68e062cac53`
📥 Commits Reviewing files that changed from the base of the PR and between 7b27c449f69547c6c385102a7938247c845a35c3 and 160da4616424fcd89a87de5abc59515ea358df10.
📒 Files selected for processing (5) * `packages/client/src/components/Map/AirportPopup.tsx` * `packages/client/src/hooks/useTafs.ts` * `packages/server/src/index.ts` * `packages/server/src/services/__tests__/taf-poller.test.ts` * `packages/server/src/services/taf-poller.ts`
🚧 Files skipped from review as they are similar to previous changes (5) * packages/server/src/index.ts * packages/client/src/hooks/useTafs.ts * packages/client/src/components/Map/AirportPopup.tsx * packages/server/src/services/taf-poller.ts * packages/server/src/services/__tests__/taf-poller.test.ts
---
📝 Walkthrough ## Walkthrough This PR adds Terminal Aerodrome Forecast (TAF) data support to the application. It defines shared data types, implements server-side polling and parsing of aviationweather.gov TAF JSON, exposes a `/airspace/taf` API endpoint, adds client-side TAF fetching hooks and period selection logic, and extends the airport popup UI to display active TAF forecasts alongside existing METAR reports. ## Changes **TAF Feature Implementation** |Layer / File(s)|Summary| |---|---| |**Shared Type Definitions**
`packages/shared/src/types/taf.ts`, `packages/shared/src/index.ts`|Introduces `TafPeriod`, `TafReport`, and `TafsResponse` interfaces modeling forecast periods with time bounds, change-group semantics, optional wind/visibility/ceiling, and multi-airport snapshots with live/cached provenance.| |**Server TAF Parser & Polling**
`packages/server/src/services/taf-poller.ts`|Parses upstream aviationweather.gov TAF JSON records into typed `TafsResponse`, normalizing visibility strings (`P6SM`, `6+`, fractions), wind directions (`VRB`), and ceiling from cloud layers; fetches with timeout/retry/backoff; maintains in-memory snapshot with cache fallback; exposes `startTafPolling(intervalMs)`, `stopTafPolling()`, `getCurrentTafSnapshot()`, and `_resetTafCache()` for testing.| |**Server API Endpoint**
`packages/server/src/routes/airspace.ts`|New `GET /airspace/taf` route returns cached TAF snapshot with 5-minute cache headers or HTTP 503 when unavailable.| |**Server Initialization**
`packages/server/src/index.ts`|Adds `TAF_POLL_INTERVAL_MS` env config (default 30 min) and calls `startTafPolling()` when `AIRSPACE_ENABLED` is true.| |**Client TAF Fetching Hook**
`packages/client/src/hooks/useTafs.ts`|New `useTafs(enabled)` hook polls `/airspace/taf` every 10 minutes when enabled, returning `{ reports, source, error }` and treating API 503 as "no reports yet" rather than error; guards against post-unmount state updates.| |**TAF Period Selection Utility**
`packages/client/src/lib/tafCurrentPeriod.ts`|Helper `findCurrentTafPeriod(forecasts, now)` selects the active base TAF period (null/FM groups) at a timestamp, ignoring TEMPO overlays, falling back to first period when `now` precedes all starts.| |**Client TAF API**
`packages/client/src/lib/api.ts`|Adds `fetchTafs()` function to retrieve TAF snapshot from `/airspace/taf` endpoint.| |**Airport Popup TAF Display**
`packages/client/src/components/Map/AirportPopup.tsx`|Extends `AirportPopup` with optional `taf: TafReport \| null` prop and conditionally renders `TafSection` showing issued/valid times, expandable raw TAF text, and active-period summary (wind/visibility/ceiling/weather) using period-selection helper and formatting utilities.| |**Transit Map TAF Integration**
`packages/client/src/components/Map/TransitMap.tsx`|Adds `tafReports: Record` prop to `TransitMapProps`; forwards selected airport's TAF to `AirportPopup` keyed by ICAO.| |**App Component TAF Wiring**
`packages/client/src/App.tsx`|Calls `useTafs(view === "airspace")` to conditionally fetch TAF data and passes `tafReports` map to `TransitMap`.| |**Server Parser Tests**
`packages/server/src/services/__tests__/taf-poller.test.ts`|Comprehensive Vitest suite for `parseTafResponse` covering ICAO-keyed reports, time conversion, invalid record filtering, `sourceTimestamp` selection from latest issue time, forecast parsing (FM/PROB/wind/visibility/ceiling/weather), and cloud-layer ceiling derivation.| |**Client Period Selection Tests**
`packages/client/src/lib/__tests__/tafCurrentPeriod.test.ts`|Vitest suite for `findCurrentTafPeriod` verifying null returns, base-period selection ignoring overlays, and boundary behavior before/after TAF validity.| |**E2E Fixtures & Mocks**
`packages/e2e/fixtures/upstream/aviationweather-taf.json`, `packages/e2e/fixtures/upstream/refresh.sh`, `packages/server/src/test-mocks.ts`|Adds sample TAF fixture for multiple ICAOs; updates fixture refresh script to download TAF data in parallel; extends test-mocks with MSW handler for aviationweather TAF endpoint.| |**Docker Build Fix**
`Dockerfile`|Adds second `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile` after copying workspace source to regenerate per-package `node_modules` symlinks for tool resolution.| ## Estimated code review effort 🎯 3 (Moderate) | ⏱️ ~25 minutes ## Possibly related PRs - [cjunks94/panoptrain#54](https://github.com/cjunks94/panoptrain/pull/54): Modifies TransitMap component; touches same UI wiring updated here. - [cjunks94/panoptrain#41](https://github.com/cjunks94/panoptrain/pull/41): Updates e2e fixtures and test-mocks; related to the fixture/mocks changes in this PR. - [cjunks94/panoptrain#55](https://github.com/cjunks94/panoptrain/pull/55): Contains the same Dockerfile pnpm install layer change. ## Poem > 🐰 I hopped through JSON, winds, and time, > Parsed every period, made summaries fine. > Polls tick and caches hum in a row, > Popups show forecasts where airplanes go. > Hooray — TAFs now dance on the map, bravo!
🚥 Pre-merge checks | ✅ 4 | ❌ 1 ### ❌ Failed checks (1 warning) | Check name | Status | Explanation | Resolution | | :----------------: | :--------- | :------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | :--------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Docstring Coverage | ⚠️ Warning | Docstring coverage is 15.00% which is insufficient. The required threshold is 80.00%. | Write docstrings for the functions missing them to satisfy the coverage threshold. |
✅ Passed checks (4 passed) | Check name | Status | Explanation | | :------------------------: | :------- | :---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Description Check | ✅ Passed | Check skipped - CodeRabbit’s high-level summary is enabled. | | Title check | ✅ Passed | The title clearly and accurately summarizes the main change: adding TAF forecasts to the airport popup component in both the API and UI layers. | | Linked Issues check | ✅ Passed | Check skipped because no linked issues were found for this pull request. | | Out of Scope Changes check | ✅ Passed | Check skipped because no linked issues were found for this pull request. |
✏️ Tip: You can configure your own custom pre-merge checks in the settings.
✨ Finishing Touches
📝 Generate docstrings - [ ] Create stacked PR - [ ] Commit on current branch
🧪 Generate unit tests (beta) - [ ] Create PR with unit tests - [ ] Commit unit tests in branch `feature/taf-forecast`

reviews.body:
**Actionable comments posted: 4**
🧹 Nitpick comments (2)
packages/client/src/lib/tafCurrentPeriod.ts (1)
`27-30`: _⚡ Quick win_ **Make active-period selection order-independent.** Line 27–30 currently depends on `forecasts` being pre-sorted. Select by max `timeFrom <= now` instead so behavior is deterministic even if upstream order varies.
Suggested patch ```diff - let active: TafPeriod | null = null; - for (const p of basePeriods) { - if (p.timeFrom <= now) active = p; - } + let active: TafPeriod | null = null; + for (const p of basePeriods) { + if (p.timeFrom <= now && (!active || p.timeFrom > active.timeFrom)) { + active = p; + } + } return active ?? basePeriods[0]; ```
🤖 Prompt for AI Agents ``` Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate. In `@packages/client/src/lib/tafCurrentPeriod.ts` around lines 27 - 30, The current selection in tafCurrentPeriod.ts uses a left-to-right overwrite loop over basePeriods (variable names basePeriods, active, now) which assumes input order; change it to pick the period with the largest timeFrom that is <= now so selection is order-independent: filter basePeriods for p.timeFrom <= now and then choose the element with the maximum timeFrom (or fallback to basePeriods[0] if none), replacing the loop that assigns active; ensure you update only the selection logic around the active variable so callers of tafCurrentPeriod.ts behave the same. ```
packages/server/src/services/taf-poller.ts (1)
`78-83`: _⚡ Quick win_ **Handle numeric-string wind directions defensively.** Line 81–82 nulls all string `wdir` values. If upstream sends `"090"` style strings, direction is dropped unnecessarily.
Suggested patch ```diff function parseDirection(wdir: AwxTafForecast["wdir"]): number | null { if (wdir === undefined || wdir === null) return null; if (typeof wdir === "number") return wdir; - // "VRB" and other strings — null is the right "no fixed direction" signal. - return null; + const trimmed = wdir.trim().toUpperCase(); + if (trimmed === "VRB") return null; + const parsed = Number(trimmed); + return Number.isFinite(parsed) ? parsed : null; } ```
🤖 Prompt for AI Agents ``` Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate. In `@packages/server/src/services/taf-poller.ts` around lines 78 - 83, The parseDirection function currently treats all string wdir values as null; update parseDirection(AwxTafForecast["wdir"]) to defensively accept numeric strings like "090" by trimming the string and converting it to a number (e.g., via Number or parseInt), validating the result is a finite number within the valid bearing range (0–360), and returning that number; continue returning null for undefined/null inputs and for non-numeric strings such as "VRB" or invalid parses (NaN/out-of-range). ```
🤖 Prompt for all review comments with AI agents ``` Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate. Inline comments: In `@packages/client/src/components/Map/AirportPopup.tsx`: - Around line 287-292: The issue is that Date.now() is being called on every render so `current = findCurrentTafPeriod(taf.forecasts, now)` can change when the component re-renders; fix by computing and storing a single pinned timestamp at mount (e.g., use a stable value via useRef or a lazy useState initializer) and use that pinned value instead of calling Date.now() directly; update the variable name (e.g., `pinnedNow`) and pass it into `findCurrentTafPeriod(taf.forecasts, pinnedNow)` so the active period remains fixed for the popup session while keeping `expanded` and its setter unchanged. In `@packages/client/src/hooks/useTafs.ts`: - Around line 39-63: The poll function can start a new fetchTafs before the previous one completes causing older responses to overwrite newer state; modify poll (and the surrounding setup using POLL_INTERVAL_MS, cancelled, setReports, setSource, setError) to prevent overlapping requests by tracking in-flight requests (e.g., a "running" boolean or incrementing requestId) or by using an AbortController to cancel the prior fetch before starting a new one, and ensure only the latest response updates state (check running/reqId or aborted signal and return early if not current). In `@packages/server/src/index.ts`: - Line 35: The code reads TAF_POLL_INTERVAL_MS into TAF_POLL_INTERVAL without validating, which allows NaN, zero, or negative values to cause aggressive polling; update the parsing for process.env.TAF_POLL_INTERVAL_MS so you validate the result (use Number.isFinite / !Number.isNaN after parseInt or Number()), enforce a sensible minimum (e.g., MIN_TAF_POLL_INTERVAL_MS = 1000 or 60000) and fall back to the existing default (1800000) when the parsed value is invalid or below the minimum; update the const TAF_POLL_INTERVAL initialization to perform this validation and clamp so downstream code using TAF_POLL_INTERVAL never gets NaN/0/negative values. In `@packages/server/src/services/taf-poller.ts`: - Around line 88-93: The current loop over clouds returns the first BKN/OVC/VV layer instead of the lowest ceiling; change the logic in the loop that uses cover and layer.base (the block starting with "for (const layer of clouds)" and variables cover and layer.base) to collect all layers where (layer.cover ?? "").toUpperCase() is "BKN"|"OVC"|"VV" and layer.base is a number, then compute and return the minimum numeric base (or null if none) instead of returning on the first match; ensure you replace the early return with a min calculation over the filtered bases. --- Nitpick comments: In `@packages/client/src/lib/tafCurrentPeriod.ts`: - Around line 27-30: The current selection in tafCurrentPeriod.ts uses a left-to-right overwrite loop over basePeriods (variable names basePeriods, active, now) which assumes input order; change it to pick the period with the largest timeFrom that is <= now so selection is order-independent: filter basePeriods for p.timeFrom <= now and then choose the element with the maximum timeFrom (or fallback to basePeriods[0] if none), replacing the loop that assigns active; ensure you update only the selection logic around the active variable so callers of tafCurrentPeriod.ts behave the same. In `@packages/server/src/services/taf-poller.ts`: - Around line 78-83: The parseDirection function currently treats all string wdir values as null; update parseDirection(AwxTafForecast["wdir"]) to defensively accept numeric strings like "090" by trimming the string and converting it to a number (e.g., via Number or parseInt), validating the result is a finite number within the valid bearing range (0–360), and returning that number; continue returning null for undefined/null inputs and for non-numeric strings such as "VRB" or invalid parses (NaN/out-of-range). ```
🪄 Autofix (Beta) Fix all unresolved CodeRabbit comments on this PR: - [ ] Push a commit to this branch (recommended) - [ ] Create a new PR with the fixes
---
